# Portainer EE 2.42.0 operation index — generated by `make specs`; do not edit.
# Format: METHOD /path → chunk file — summary
POST /auth → auth.yaml — Authenticate
POST /auth/logout → auth.yaml — Logout
POST /auth/oauth/validate → auth.yaml — Authenticate with OAuth
GET /auto_updates → auto_updates.yaml — List auto updates
POST /backup → backup.yaml — Creates an archive with a system data snapshot that could be used to restore the system.
POST /backup/azure/execute → backup.yaml — Execute an ad-hoc backup to Azure Blob Storage
POST /backup/azure/restore → backup.yaml — Restore system state from an Azure Blob Storage backup
GET /backup/azure/settings → backup.yaml — Fetch Azure Blob Storage backup settings
POST /backup/azure/settings → backup.yaml — Update Azure Blob Storage backup settings and reschedule cron job
GET /backup/azure/status → backup.yaml — Fetch the status of the last scheduled Azure Blob backup run
POST /backup/azure/test → backup.yaml — Test connectivity to an Azure Blob Storage container
POST /backup/local/run → backup.yaml — Trigger an immediate local backup outside of the cron schedule
GET /backup/local/settings → backup.yaml — Fetch local scheduled backup settings
POST /backup/local/settings → backup.yaml — Updates local scheduled backup settings and restarts the cron job as needed
GET /backup/local/status → backup.yaml — Fetch the status of the last scheduled local backup run
POST /backup/s3/execute → backup.yaml — Execute backup to AWS S3 Bucket
POST /backup/s3/restore → backup.yaml — Triggers a system restore using details of s3 backup
GET /backup/s3/settings → backup.yaml — Fetch s3 backup settings/configurations
POST /backup/s3/settings → backup.yaml — Updates stored s3 backup settings and updates running cron jobs as needed
GET /backup/s3/status → backup.yaml — Fetch the status of the last scheduled backup run
POST /cloud/amazon/provision → kaas.yaml — Provision a new Amazon EKS and create an environment
POST /cloud/azure/provision → kaas.yaml — Provision a new Microsoft Azure cluster and create an environment
GET /cloud/credentials → cloud_credentials.yaml — getAll cloud credentials
POST /cloud/credentials → cloud_credentials.yaml — Create a cloud credential
GET /cloud/credentials/{id} → cloud_credentials.yaml — getByID gets a cloud credential by ID
PUT /cloud/credentials/{id} → cloud_credentials.yaml — Update a cloud credential
DELETE /cloud/credentials/{id} → cloud_credentials.yaml — Delete a cloud credential
POST /cloud/endpoints/{environmentId}/upgrade → kaas.yaml — Upgrade the cluster to the next stable kubernetes version.
GET /cloud/gitcredentials → cloud_credentials.yaml — Get Shared Git Credentials
POST /cloud/gitcredentials → cloud_credentials.yaml — Create Shared Git Credential
GET /cloud/gitcredentials/{id} → cloud_credentials.yaml — Get Shared Git Credential
PUT /cloud/gitcredentials/{id} → cloud_credentials.yaml — Update a Shared Git Credential
DELETE /cloud/gitcredentials/{id} → cloud_credentials.yaml — Delete Shared Git Credential
POST /cloud/gke/provision → kaas.yaml — Provision a new Google Kubernetes (GKE) cluster and create an environment
GET /cloud/{provider}/info → kaas.yaml — Get information about the provisioning options for a cloud provider.
POST /cloud/{provider}/provision → kaas.yaml — Provision a new CIVO, Linode or Digital Ocean cluster and create an environment
GET /custom_templates → custom_templates.yaml — List available custom templates
POST /custom_templates → custom_templates.yaml — Create a custom template
POST /custom_templates/create/file → custom_templates.yaml — Create a custom template
POST /custom_templates/create/repository → custom_templates.yaml — Create a custom template
POST /custom_templates/create/string → custom_templates.yaml — Create a custom template
GET /custom_templates/{id} → custom_templates.yaml — Inspect a custom template
PUT /custom_templates/{id} → custom_templates.yaml — Update a template
DELETE /custom_templates/{id} → custom_templates.yaml — Remove a template
GET /custom_templates/{id}/file → custom_templates.yaml — Get Template stack file content.
PUT /custom_templates/{id}/git_fetch → custom_templates.yaml — Fetch the latest config file content based on custom template's git repository configuration
POST /docker/{environmentId}/containers/image_status/clear → docker.yaml — Clear container image status cache
GET /docker/{environmentId}/containers/{containerId}/gpus → docker.yaml — Fetch container gpus data
GET /docker/{environmentId}/containers/{containerId}/image_status → docker.yaml — Fetch image status for container
GET /docker/{environmentId}/dashboard → docker.yaml — Get counters for the dashboard
GET /docker/{environmentId}/images → docker.yaml — Fetch images
POST /docker/{environmentId}/services/image_status/clear → docker.yaml — Clear service image status cache
GET /docker/{environmentId}/services/{serviceId}/image_status → docker.yaml — Fetch image status for service
GET /docker/{environmentId}/snapshot → endpoints.yaml — Fetch latest snapshot of environment
GET /docker/{environmentId}/snapshot/containers → endpoints.yaml — Fetch containers list from a snapshot
GET /docker/{environmentId}/snapshot/containers/{containerId} → endpoints.yaml — Fetch container from a snapshot
GET /edge_configurations → edge_configs.yaml — List available Edge Configurations
POST /edge_configurations → edge_configs.yaml — Create an Edge Configuration
GET /edge_configurations/{id} → edge_configs.yaml — Inspect an Edge configuration
PUT /edge_configurations/{id} → edge_configs.yaml — Update an Edge Configuration
DELETE /edge_configurations/{id} → edge_configs.yaml — Delete an Edge configuration
GET /edge_configurations/{id}/files → edge_configs.yaml — Get the files for an Edge configuration
PUT /edge_configurations/{id}/{state} → edge_configs.yaml — Update the state of an Edge configuration
GET /edge_groups → edge_groups.yaml — list EdgeGroups
POST /edge_groups → edge_groups.yaml — Create an EdgeGroup
GET /edge_groups/{id} → edge_groups.yaml — Inspects an EdgeGroup
PUT /edge_groups/{id} → edge_groups.yaml — Updates an EdgeGroup
DELETE /edge_groups/{id} → edge_groups.yaml — Deletes an EdgeGroup
GET /edge_jobs → edge_jobs.yaml — Fetch EdgeJobs list
POST /edge_jobs/create/file → edge_jobs.yaml — Create an EdgeJob from a file
POST /edge_jobs/create/string → edge_jobs.yaml — Create an EdgeJob from a text
GET /edge_jobs/{id} → edge_jobs.yaml — Inspect an EdgeJob
PUT /edge_jobs/{id} → edge_jobs.yaml — Update an EdgeJob
DELETE /edge_jobs/{id} → edge_jobs.yaml — Delete an EdgeJob
GET /edge_jobs/{id}/file → edge_jobs.yaml — Fetch a file of an EdgeJob
GET /edge_jobs/{id}/tasks → edge_jobs.yaml — Fetch the list of tasks on an EdgeJob
GET /edge_jobs/{id}/tasks/{taskID}/logs → edge_jobs.yaml — Fetch the log for a specifc task on an EdgeJob
POST /edge_jobs/{id}/tasks/{taskID}/logs → edge_jobs.yaml — Collect the log for a specifc task on an EdgeJob
DELETE /edge_jobs/{id}/tasks/{taskID}/logs → edge_jobs.yaml — Clear the log for a specifc task on an EdgeJob
GET /edge_stacks → edge_stacks.yaml — Fetches the list of EdgeStacks
POST /edge_stacks/create/file → edge_stacks.yaml — Create an EdgeStack from file
POST /edge_stacks/create/helmRepo → edge_stacks.yaml — Create an EdgeStack from a Helm repository
POST /edge_stacks/create/repository → edge_stacks.yaml — Create an EdgeStack from a git repository
POST /edge_stacks/create/string → edge_stacks.yaml — Create an EdgeStack from a text
POST /edge_stacks/parse_registries → edge_stacks.yaml — Parse registries from a stack file
POST /edge_stacks/webhooks/{webhookID} → stacks.yaml — Webhook for triggering edge stack updates from git
GET /edge_stacks/{id} → edge_stacks.yaml — Inspect an EdgeStack
PUT /edge_stacks/{id} → edge_stacks.yaml — Update an EdgeStack
DELETE /edge_stacks/{id} → edge_stacks.yaml — Delete an EdgeStack
GET /edge_stacks/{id}/file → edge_stacks.yaml — Fetches the stack file for an EdgeStack
PUT /edge_stacks/{id}/git → edge_stacks.yaml — Update git configuration and pulls the repository
PUT /edge_stacks/{id}/helmRepo → edge_stacks.yaml — Update an EdgeStack from a Helm repository
GET /edge_stacks/{id}/logs/{endpoint_id} → edge_stacks.yaml — Gets the status of the log collection for a given edgestack and environment
PUT /edge_stacks/{id}/logs/{endpoint_id} → edge_stacks.yaml — Schedule the collection of logs for a given endpoint and edge stack
DELETE /edge_stacks/{id}/logs/{endpoint_id} → edge_stacks.yaml — Deletes the available logs for a given edge stack and endpoint
GET /edge_stacks/{id}/logs/{endpoint_id}/file → edge_stacks.yaml — Downloads the available logs for a given edge stack and endpoint
GET /edge_stacks/{id}/stagger/status → edge_stacks.yaml — Inspect an EdgeStack's parallel update status
PUT /edge_stacks/{id}/status → edge_stacks.yaml — Update an EdgeStack status
GET /edge_update_schedules → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Fetches the list of Edge Update Schedules
POST /edge_update_schedules → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Creates a scheduled remote update procedure for Edge agents
POST /edge_update_schedules/active → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Fetches the list of Active Edge Update Schedules
GET /edge_update_schedules/agent_versions → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Fetches the supported versions of the agent to update/rollback
GET /edge_update_schedules/info → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Returns informations the help create edge update schedules
GET /edge_update_schedules/previous_versions → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Fetches the previous versions of updated agents
GET /edge_update_schedules/{id} → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Returns the Edge Update Schedule with the given ID
POST /edge_update_schedules/{id} → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Update a scheduled remote update procedure for Edge agents
DELETE /edge_update_schedules/{id} → edge_update_schedules.yaml — Deletes an Edge Update Schedule
GET /endpoint_groups → endpoint_groups.yaml — List Environment(Endpoint) groups
POST /endpoint_groups → endpoint_groups.yaml — Create an Environment(Endpoint) Group
GET /endpoint_groups/{id} → endpoint_groups.yaml — Inspect an Environment(Endpoint) group
PUT /endpoint_groups/{id} → endpoint_groups.yaml — Update an environment(endpoint) group
DELETE /endpoint_groups/{id} → endpoint_groups.yaml — Remove an environment(endpoint) group
PUT /endpoint_groups/{id}/endpoints/{endpointId} → endpoint_groups.yaml — Add an environment(endpoint) to an environment(endpoint) group
DELETE /endpoint_groups/{id}/endpoints/{endpointId} → endpoint_groups.yaml — Removes environment(endpoint) from an environment(endpoint) group
GET /endpoints → endpoints.yaml — List environments(endpoints)
POST /endpoints → endpoints.yaml — Create a new environment(endpoint)
DELETE /endpoints → endpoints.yaml — Remove multiple environments
GET /endpoints/agent_versions → endpoints.yaml — List agent versions
POST /endpoints/delete → endpoints.yaml — Remove multiple environments
POST /endpoints/edge/async → endpoints.yaml — Get environment(endpoint) status
POST /endpoints/edge/generate-key → edge.yaml — Generate an EdgeKey
POST /endpoints/edge/trust → endpoints.yaml — Associate one or more Edge environments in the waiting room to environments
POST /endpoints/global-key → endpoints.yaml — Create or retrieve the endpoint for an EdgeID
PUT /endpoints/relations → endpoints.yaml — Update relations for a list of environments
POST /endpoints/snapshot → endpoints.yaml — Snapshot all environment(endpoint)
GET /endpoints/summary → endpoints.yaml — Get environment summary counts
GET /endpoints/{id} → endpoints.yaml — Inspect an environment(endpoint)
PUT /endpoints/{id} → endpoints.yaml — Update an environment(endpoint)
DELETE /endpoints/{id} → endpoints.yaml — Remove an environment
PUT /endpoints/{id}/association → endpoints.yaml — De-association an edge environment(endpoint)
POST /endpoints/{id}/docker/v2/browse/put → endpoints.yaml — Upload a file under a specific path on the file system of an environment (endpoint)
GET /endpoints/{id}/dockerhub/{registryId} → endpoints.yaml — fetch docker pull limits
POST /endpoints/{id}/edge/alerts → edge.yaml — Receive Alertmanager-compatible alerts from an Edge agent

POST /endpoints/{id}/edge/jobs/{jobID}/logs → edge.yaml — Update the logs collected from an Edge Job

GET /endpoints/{id}/edge/status → endpoints.yaml — Get environment(endpoint) status
PUT /endpoints/{id}/forceupdateservice → endpoints.yaml — force update a docker service
GET /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm → helm.yaml — List Helm Releases
POST /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm → helm.yaml — Install Helm Chart
POST /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm/git/dryrun → helm.yaml — Helm Git Dry Run
GET /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm/{release} → helm.yaml — Get a helm release
DELETE /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm/{release} → helm.yaml — Delete Helm Release
GET /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm/{release}/history → helm.yaml — Get a historical list of releases
POST /endpoints/{id}/kubernetes/helm/{release}/rollback → helm.yaml — Rollback a helm release
GET /endpoints/{id}/mtls_certificate → endpoints.yaml — Get an environment(endpoint) mTLS certificate
GET /endpoints/{id}/mtls_certificate_error → endpoints.yaml — Get an agent(endpoint) mTLS certificate
PUT /endpoints/{id}/pools/{rpn}/access → endpoints.yaml — Update namespace access for a given namespace
GET /endpoints/{id}/registries → endpoints.yaml — List Registries on environment
PUT /endpoints/{id}/registries/{registryId} → endpoints.yaml — update registry access for environment
PUT /endpoints/{id}/settings → endpoints.yaml — Update settings for an environment(endpoint)
POST /endpoints/{id}/snapshot → endpoints.yaml — Snapshots an environment(endpoint)
POST /gitops/repo/file/preview → gitops.yaml — preview the content of target file in the git repository
POST /gitops/repo/files/search → gitops.yaml — Search the file path from a git repository files with specified extensions
POST /gitops/repo/helm/values → gitops.yaml — Preview merged Helm values files from git repository
POST /gitops/repo/refs → gitops.yaml — List the refs of a git repository
GET /gitops/sources → gitops.yaml — List all GitOps sources
GET /gitops/sources/summary → gitops.yaml — Summarize GitOps source status counts
GET /gitops/sources/{id} → gitops.yaml — Get a GitOps source by ID
GET /gitops/workflows → gitops.yaml — List all GitOps workflows
GET /gitops/workflows/summary → gitops.yaml — Summarize GitOps workflow status counts

PUT /kubernetes/{environmentId}/opa → kubernetes.yaml — Update Pod Security Rule within k8s cluster
GET /kubernetes/{id}/applications → kubernetes.yaml — Get a list of applications across all namespaces in the cluster. If the nodeName is provided, it will return the applications running on that node.
GET /kubernetes/{id}/applications/count → kubernetes.yaml — Get Applications count
POST /kubernetes/{id}/cluster_role_bindings/delete → kubernetes.yaml — Delete cluster role bindings
POST /kubernetes/{id}/cluster_roles/delete → kubernetes.yaml — Delete cluster roles
GET /kubernetes/{id}/clusterrolebindings → kubernetes.yaml — Get a list of kubernetes cluster role bindings
GET /kubernetes/{id}/clusterroles → kubernetes.yaml — Get a list of kubernetes cluster roles

GET /kubernetes/{id}/configmaps/count → kubernetes.yaml — Get ConfigMaps count

POST /kubernetes/{id}/ingresses/delete → kubernetes.yaml — Delete one or more Ingresses

PUT /settings/additional_functionality → settings.yaml — Update Portainer additional functionality settings
PUT /settings/default_registry → settings.yaml — Update Portainer default registry settings
GET /settings/edge/mtls_ca_certificate → settings.yaml — Retrieve Portainer Edge MTLS CA Certificates
GET /settings/edge/mtls_certificate → settings.yaml — Retrieve Portainer Edge MTLS Certificates
GET /settings/experimental → settings.yaml — Retrieve Portainer experimental settings
PUT /settings/experimental → settings.yaml — Update Portainer experimental settings
GET /settings/public → settings.yaml — Retrieve Portainer public settings
POST /sshkeygen → cloud_credentials.yaml — Generate ssh keypair
GET /ssl → ssl.yaml — Inspect the ssl settings
PUT /ssl → ssl.yaml — Update the ssl settings
GET /stacks → stacks.yaml — List stacks
POST /stacks/create/kubernetes/repository → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new kubernetes stack from a git repository
POST /stacks/create/kubernetes/string → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new kubernetes stack from a file
POST /stacks/create/kubernetes/url → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new kubernetes stack from a url
POST /stacks/create/standalone/file → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new compose stack from a file
POST /stacks/create/standalone/repository → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new compose stack from repository
POST /stacks/create/standalone/string → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new compose stack from a text
POST /stacks/create/swarm/file → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new swarm stack from a file
POST /stacks/create/swarm/repository → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new swarm stack from a git repository
POST /stacks/create/swarm/string → stacks.yaml — Deploy a new swarm stack from a text
POST /stacks/image_status/clear → docker.yaml — Clear stack image status cache
DELETE /stacks/name/{name} → stacks.yaml — Remove Kubernetes stacks by name
POST /stacks/webhooks/{webhookID} → stacks.yaml — Webhook for triggering stack updates from git
GET /stacks/{id} → stacks.yaml — Inspect a stack
PUT /stacks/{id} → stacks.yaml — Update a stack
DELETE /stacks/{id} → stacks.yaml — Remove a stack
PUT /stacks/{id}/associate → stacks.yaml — Associate an orphaned stack to a new environment(endpoint)
POST /stacks/{id}/convert → stacks.yaml — Convert a Docker Compose stack to Kubernetes manifests or Helm chart
GET /stacks/{id}/file → stacks.yaml — Retrieve the content of the Stack file for the specified stack
POST /stacks/{id}/git → stacks.yaml — Update a stack's Git configs
PUT /stacks/{id}/git/redeploy → stacks.yaml — Redeploy a stack
GET /stacks/{id}/images_status → stacks.yaml — Fetch image status for stack
POST /stacks/{id}/migrate → stacks.yaml — Migrate a stack to another environment(endpoint)
POST /stacks/{id}/start → stacks.yaml — Starts a stopped Stack
POST /stacks/{id}/stop → stacks.yaml — Stops a stopped Stack
GET /status → status.yaml — Check Portainer status
GET /support/debug_log → support.yaml — Get status of the global debug log
PUT /support/debug_log → support.yaml — Enables or disables the global debug log
POST /support/download → support.yaml — Download a bundle of files to help support diagnose issues
GET /system/info → system.yaml — Retrieve system info
GET /system/nodes → system.yaml — Retrieve the count of nodes
GET /system/status → system.yaml — Check Portainer status
POST /system/update → system.yaml — Update Portainer to latest version
GET /system/version → system.yaml — Check for portainer updates
GET /tags → tags.yaml — List tags
POST /tags → tags.yaml — Create a new tag
DELETE /tags/{id} → tags.yaml — Remove a tag
GET /team_memberships → team_memberships.yaml — List team memberships
POST /team_memberships → team_memberships.yaml — Create a new team membership
PUT /team_memberships/{id} → team_memberships.yaml — Update a team membership
DELETE /team_memberships/{id} → team_memberships.yaml — Remove a team membership
GET /teams → teams.yaml — List teams
POST /teams → teams.yaml — Create a new team
GET /teams/{id} → teams.yaml — Inspect a team
PUT /teams/{id} → teams.yaml — Update a team
DELETE /teams/{id} → teams.yaml — Remove a team
GET /teams/{id}/memberships → team_memberships.yaml — List team memberships
GET /templates → templates.yaml — List available templates
GET /templates/helm → helm.yaml — Search Helm Charts
GET /templates/helm/{command} → helm.yaml — Show Helm Chart Information
POST /templates/{id}/file → templates.yaml — Get a template's file
POST /upload/tls/{certificate} → upload.yaml — Upload TLS files
GET /useractivity/authlogs → useractivity.yaml — List auth activity logs
GET /useractivity/authlogs.csv → useractivity.yaml — Download auth logs as CSV
GET /useractivity/logs → useractivity.yaml — List user activity logs
GET /useractivity/logs.csv → useractivity.yaml — Download user activity logs as CSV
GET /users → users.yaml — List users
POST /users → users.yaml — Create a new user
GET /users/admin/check → users.yaml — Check administrator account existence
POST /users/admin/init → users.yaml — Initialize administrator account
GET /users/me → users.yaml — Inspect the current user
GET /users/me/auth/{endpointID} → users.yaml — Inspect environment authorizations for the current user
GET /users/{id} → users.yaml — Inspect a user
PUT /users/{id} → users.yaml — Update a user
DELETE /users/{id} → users.yaml — Remove a user
GET /users/{id}/effective-access → users.yaml — Inspect a user's effective access on every environment
GET /users/{id}/gitcredentials → users.yaml — Get all saved git credentials for a user
POST /users/{id}/gitcredentials → users.yaml — Store a Git Credential for a user
GET /users/{id}/gitcredentials/{credentialID} → users.yaml — Get the specific saved git credential for a user
PUT /users/{id}/gitcredentials/{credentialID} → users.yaml — Update a git-credential associated to a user
DELETE /users/{id}/gitcredentials/{credentialID} → users.yaml — Remove a git-credential associated to a user
GET /users/{id}/helm/repositories → helm.yaml — List a users helm repositories
POST /users/{id}/helm/repositories → helm.yaml — Create a user helm repository
DELETE /users/{id}/helm/repositories/{repositoryID} → helm.yaml — Delete a users helm repository
GET /users/{id}/memberships → users.yaml — Inspect a user memberships
GET /users/{id}/namespaces → users.yaml — Retrieves all k8s namespaces for an user
PUT /users/{id}/passwd → users.yaml — Update password for a user
GET /users/{id}/tokens → users.yaml — Get all API keys for a user
POST /users/{id}/tokens → users.yaml — Generate an API key for a user
DELETE /users/{id}/tokens/{keyID} → users.yaml — Remove an api-key associated to a user
GET /webhooks → webhooks.yaml — List webhooks
POST /webhooks → webhooks.yaml — Create a webhook
PUT /webhooks/{id} → webhooks.yaml — Update a webhook
POST /webhooks/{id} → webhooks.yaml — Execute a webhook
DELETE /webhooks/{id} → webhooks.yaml — Delete a webhook
PUT /webhooks/{id}/reassign → webhooks.yaml — Reassign a webhook to another resource
GET /websocket/attach → websocket.yaml — Attach a websocket
GET /websocket/exec → websocket.yaml — Execute a websocket
GET /websocket/kubernetes-shell → websocket.yaml — Execute a websocket on kubectl shell pod
GET /websocket/pod → websocket.yaml — Execute a websocket on pod
